delete_comment.description: Deletes a specific comment. Only the person who made the comment is allowed to delete it.

comment_id.description: Comment id of comment to delete

file_key.description: File to delete comment from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

delete_comment_reaction.description: Deletes a specific comment reaction. Only the person who made the reaction is allowed to delete it.

comment_id.description: ID of comment to delete reaction from.

file_key.description: File to delete comment reaction from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

delete_dev_resource.description: Delete a dev resource from a file

dev_resource_id.description: The id of the dev resource to delete.

file_key.description: The file to delete the dev resource from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key.

delete_webhook.description: Deletes the specified webhook. This operation cannot be reversed.

get_activity_logs.description: Returns a list of activity log events

end_time.description: Unix timestamp of the most recent event to include. This param defaults to the current timestamp if unspecified.

events.description: Event type(s) to include in the response. Can have multiple values separated by comma. All events are returned by default.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return. This param defaults to 1000 if unspecified.

order.description: Event order by timestamp. This param can be either "asc" (default) or "desc".

start_time.description: Unix timestamp of the least recent event to include. This param defaults to one year ago if unspecified.

get_comment_reactions.description: Gets a paginated list of reactions left on the comment.

comment_id.description: ID of comment to get reactions from.

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination, retrieved from the response of the previous call.

file_key.description: File to get comment containing reactions from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

get_comments.description: Gets a list of comments left on the file.

as_md.description: If enabled, will return comments as their markdown equivalents when applicable.

file_key.description: File to get comments from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

get_component.description: Get metadata on a component by key.

key.description: The unique identifier of the component.

get_component_set.description: Get metadata on a published component set by key.

key.description: The unique identifier of the component set.

get_dev_resources.description: Get dev resources in a file

file_key.description: The file to get the dev resources from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key.

node_ids.description: Comma separated list of nodes that you care about in the document. If specified, only dev resources attached to these nodes will be returned. If not specified, all dev resources in the file will be...

file_key.description: File to export JSON from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

geometry.description: Set to "paths" to export vector data.

ids.description: Comma separated list of nodes that you care about in the document. If specified, only a subset of the document will be returned corresponding to the nodes listed, their children, and everything bet...

plugin_data.description: A comma separated list of plugin IDs and/or the string "shared". Any data present in the document written by those plugins will be included in the result in the `pluginData` and `sharedPluginData` ...

version.description: A specific version ID to get. Omitting this will get the current version of the file.

get_file_component_sets.description: Get a list of published component sets within a file library.

file_key.description: File to list component sets from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key, as it is not possible to publish from branches.

get_file_components.description: Get a list of published components within a file library.

file_key.description: File to list components from. This must be a main file key, not a branch key, as it is not possible to publish from branches.

get_file_nodes.description: Returns the nodes referenced to by `ids` as a JSON object. The nodes are retrieved from the Figma file referenced to by `file_key`. The node ID and file key can be parsed from any Figma node url: `...

depth.description: Positive integer representing how deep into the node tree to traverse. For example, setting this to 1 will return only the children directly underneath the desired nodes. Not setting this parameter...

file_key.description: File to export JSON from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

geometry.description: Set to "paths" to export vector data.

ids.description: A comma separated list of node IDs to retrieve and convert.

plugin_data.description: A comma separated list of plugin IDs and/or the string "shared". Any data present in the document written by those plugins will be included in the result in the `pluginData` and `sharedPluginData` ...

version.description: A specific version ID to get. Omitting this will get the current version of the file.

get_file_styles.description: Get a list of published styles within a file library.

get_file_versions.description: This endpoint fetches the version history of a file, allowing you to see the progression of a file over time. You can then use this information to render a specific version of the file, via another...

after.description: A version ID for one of the versions in the history. Gets versions after this ID. Used for paginating. If the response is not paginated, this property is not included.

before.description: A version ID for one of the versions in the history. Gets versions before this ID. Used for paginating. If the response is not paginated, this link returns the same data in the current response.

file_key.description: File to get version history from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

page_size.description: The number of items returned in a page of the response. If not included, `page_size` is `30`.

get_image_fills.description: Returns download links for all images present in image fills in a document. Image fills are how Figma represents any user supplied images. When you drag an image into Figma, we create a rectangle w...

file_key.description: File to get image URLs from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

get_images.description: Renders images from a file. If no error occurs, `"images"` will be populated with a map from node IDs to URLs of the rendered images, and `"status"` will be omitted. The image assets will expire af...

contents_only.description: Whether content that overlaps the node should be excluded from rendering. Passing false (i.e., rendering overlaps) may increase processing time, since more of the document must be included in rende...

file_key.description: File to export images from. This can be a file key or branch key. Use `GET /v1/files/:key` with the `branch_data` query param to get the branch key.

format.description: A string enum for the image output format.

ids.description: A comma separated list of node IDs to render.

scale.description: A number between 0.01 and 4, the image scaling factor.

svg_include_id.description: Whether to include id attributes for all SVG elements. Adds the layer name to the `id` attribute of an svg element.

svg_include_node_id.description: Whether to include node id attributes for all SVG elements. Adds the node id to a `data-node-id` attribute of an svg element.

svg_outline_text.description:
Whether text elements are rendered as outlines (vector paths) or as `<text>` elements in SVGs.

Rendering text elements as outlines guarantees that the text looks exactly the same in the SVG as it ...

svg_simplify_stroke.description: Whether to simplify inside/outside strokes and use stroke attribute if possible instead of `<mask>`.

use_absolute_bounds.description: Use the full dimensions of the node regardless of whether or not it is cropped or the space around it is empty. Use this to export text nodes without cropping.

version.description: A specific version ID to get. Omitting this will get the current version of the file.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) of the earliest week to include. Dates are rounded back to the nearest start of a week. Defaults to one year prior.

get_o_embed.description: Returns oEmbed data for a Figma file or published Make site URL, following the [oEmbed specification](https://oembed.com/).

maxheight.description: Maximum height of the embed in pixels. Defaults to 450. The response height will be adjusted to maintain a 16:9 aspect ratio with maxwidth.

maxwidth.description: Maximum width of the embed in pixels. Defaults to 800. The response width will be adjusted to maintain a 16:9 aspect ratio with maxheight.

url.description: The URL of the Figma file or published Make site to retrieve oEmbed data for.

get_team_component_sets.description: Get a paginated list of published component sets within a team library.

after.description: Cursor indicating which id after which to start retrieving component sets for. Exclusive with before. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids.

before.description: Cursor indicating which id before which to start retrieving component sets for. Exclusive with after. The cursor value is an internally tracked integer that doesn't correspond to any Ids.

page_size.description: Number of items to return in a paged list of results. Defaults to 30.

post_comment.display_name: Add a comment to a file
post_comment.description: Posts a new comment on the file.

client_meta.description: The position where to place the comment.

comment_id.description: The ID of the comment to reply to, if any. This must be a root comment. You cannot reply to other replies (a comment that has a parent_id).

message.description: The text contents of the comment to post.

post_comment_reaction.description: Posts a new comment reaction on a file comment.

context.description: Context to create the webhook for. Must be "team", "project", or "file".

context_id.description: The id of the context you want to receive updates about.

description.description: User provided description or name for the webhook. Max length 150 characters.

endpoint.description: The HTTP endpoint that will receive a POST request when the event triggers. Max length 2048 characters.

event_type.description: An enum representing the possible events that a webhook can subscribe to

passcode.description: String that will be passed back to your webhook endpoint to verify that it is being called by Figma. Max length 100 characters.

status.description:
An enum representing the possible statuses you can set a webhook to:
- `ACTIVE`: The webhook is healthy and receive all events
- `PAUSED`: The webhook is paused and will not receive any events

client_meta.description: Positioning information of the comment. Includes information on the location of the comment pin, which is either the absolute coordinates on the canvas or a relative offset within a frame. If the comment is a region, it will also contain the region height, width, and position of the anchor in regards to the region.

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the comment was left

order_id.description: Only set for top level comments. The number displayed with the comment in the UI

parent_id.description: If present, the id of the comment to which this is the reply

reactions.description: An array of reactions to the comment

resolved_at.description: If set, the UTC ISO 8601 time the comment was resolved

event_type.description: An enum representing the possible events that a webhook can subscribe to

passcode.description: The passcode that will be passed back to the webhook endpoint. For security, when using the GET endpoints, the value is an empty string

plan_api_id.description: The plan API ID of the team or organization where this webhook was created

status.description:
An enum representing the possible statuses you can set a webhook to:
- `ACTIVE`: The webhook is healthy and receive all events
- `PAUSED`: The webhook is paused and will not receive any events
